title: edritria digitalis
intro: Edritria digitalis, also known as foxglove, is a flowering plant that belongs to the figwort family. The medicinal properties of foxglove are derived from its leaves, which contain a group of compounds called glycosides, particularly digoxin and digitoxin. These glycosides have been used to treat heart conditions, such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, by increasing the force of the heart's contractions and regulating its rhythm. Herbal preparations of foxglove, often in the form of dried leaves or tinctures, can be used to treat various health issues, including high blood pressure, and should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
